calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Bill Swearinger appearing for petitioner Robert Foster. Kyle Childress appearing for and with trustee Hadley Hall. The Court has many questions on the proposed order. On Mr. Swearinger's Application for redaction of the record, Court can not do it pursuant to the stipulation. Court proposted to have the entire document remove from the file and Mr. Childress can refile his response with the redaction with the language that the original filing was remove pursuant to the stipulation and allowing him to refile. Fees for period after the April hearing addressed. Court can not find any statement for time services from 8/1/06 to 12/11/06. Mr. Childress will submit a declaration for his service for that period. Mr. Swearinger argued that the fees after April 28 did not benefit the trust. Mr. Childress argued that the fees are not subject to this petition and the Court should not rule on it. Court will look at Mr. Childress's fee declaration and the fees again. If the declaration is not received by the end of next week, Court will disallow the fees. Matter submitted.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Lee.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Continued To Sep-17-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Bill Sweringer appearing for and with petitioner Robert Foster. Kyle Childress appearing for and with trustee Hadley Hall. Both sides agreed on termination of trust with a $2,000.00 reserve for tax preparation and misc. out of pocket costs with any balance to be distributed to Coming Home Hospice. The trustee's accounting is not before the Court for approval. Mr. Foster has no objection to the accounting and he stipulated to no further accounting be required. The trustee consents if the trustee is relieved from future claims. Mr. Sweringer has spoken to counsel for Coming Home Hospice and they don't have any objection. He will obtain a waiver from them. Both sides stipulated to the accounting contingent upon Coming Home waiving an accounting. On attorney's fees that have been paid to Mr. Childress, Mr. Sweringer wanted the Court to determine the amount that is excessive and it was not for the benefit of the beneficiaries. Issue addressed. Attorney fees issue is submitted. Court will look at the request for trustee's fees with his attorney fees even though the request was not done procedurally. Mr. Sweringer to prepare an order to approve the termination of trust upon receipt from Coming Home Hospice to waive the accounting with a stipulation to a $3,000.00 reserve for tax preparation and out of pocket expenses and he is to leave blank on the amount of fees. He will send it to Mr. Childress before submitting it.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-25-2007 Continued To Jul-16-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-04-2007 Continued To Jun-25-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-26-2006 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Kyle Childress appearing for and with trustee Hadley Dale Hall. Bill Swearinger appearing for and with objector Robert Foster. Oral arguments presented by both sides. Court is denying the petition. Mr. Hall does have the ability to name a successor trustee. Mr. Childress to prepare an order. Mr. Swearinger can file a petition to terminate the trust. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
